sink_impls.notes: Production standard grep-style output; instantiated via Standard::sink / Standard::sink_with_path
Production JSON Lines output; instantiated via JSON::sink / JSON::sink_with_path
Production summary (count/path/quiet) output; instantiated via Summary::sink / Summary::sink_with_path
Closure wrapper; only implements matched(); ergonomic helper in the searcher crate, not used in production dispatch
Closure wrapper with lossy UTF-8 conversion; only implements matched(); convenience helper
Closure wrapper over raw bytes; only implements matched(); convenience helper
impl<S: Sink> Sink for &mut S — forwards all methods
impl<S: Sink> Sink for Box<S> — forwards all methods

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search
call_chain.notes: Entry point; routes to search_preprocessor, search_decompress, or search_path based on config

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search_path (method)
call_chain.notes: Opens file path, delegates to SearchWorker::search_reader method

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search_reader (method)
call_chain.notes: Thin wrapper; passes self.matcher, self.searcher, self.printer to free-function search_reader

call_chain.name: search_reader (free fn)
call_chain.notes: DISPATCH POINT: matches on Printer enum variants (Standard | Summary | JSON), calls printer.sink_with_path() to obtain the concrete Sink, then calls searcher.search_reader(rdr, sink)

call_chain.name: Searcher::search_reader
call_chain.notes: Core searcher method; drives the search loop, calls Sink::begin, Sink::matched, Sink::context, Sink::context_break, Sink::binary_data, Sink::finish

change_impact_map.change: Create new file. Define Config, CSVBuilder, CSV<W>, and CSVSink<'p, 's, M, W>. Implement impl Sink for CSVSink. Follow builder pattern from standard.rs: CSVBuilder::build(wtr) -> CSV<W>, CSV::sink(matcher) -> CSVSink, CSV::sink_with_path(matcher, path) -> CSVSink.
Add 'mod csv;' module declaration. Add CSV, CSVBuilder, CSVSink to the pub use re-export block alongside the existing Standard/JSON/Summary exports.
Add Csv(grep::printer::CSV<W>) variant to the Printer<W> enum.
Add Printer::Csv(ref mut p) arm to the get_mut method on SearchWorker to expose the inner writer.
Add match arm for Printer::Csv in the search_path free fn: call p.sink_with_path(matcher, path) -> sink, then searcher.search_path(path, sink).
Add match arm for Printer::Csv in the search_reader free fn: call p.sink_with_path(matcher, path) -> sink, then searcher.search_reader(rdr, sink). This is the primary dispatch point.

At the printer construction site (where Printer::Standard / Printer::JSON / Printer::Summary are selected from CLI flags), add a Printer::Csv branch for a new --output-format=csv flag and wire CSVBuilder configuration from parsed flags.
